root_causes.cause: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively shut, causing the link to go down on R2.
R1 Serial0/1/0 is administratively up but its line protocol went to lower-layer-down because the remote peer (R2) is administratively down/unavailable.
evidence: get_interface_status(router2): neighbor admin down (see above) — explains why R1's operational state dropped to lower-layer-down
get_ospf(router1): Serial0/1/0 interface_state = "Down" and no neighbors

data.intent_description: Assess recent interface operational state changes: R1 Serial0/1/0 changed from up to lower-layer-down (admin up) and R2 Serial0/1/0 changed from up to down (admin down); determine root cause and whether remediation is required.

root_causes.cause: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively shut, causing the link to go down on R2.
R1 Serial0/1/0 is administratively up but its line protocol went to lower-layer-down because the remote peer (R2) is administratively down/unavailable.
evidence: get_interface_status(router2): neighbor admin down (see above) — explains why R1's operational state dropped to lower-layer-down
get_ospf(router1): Serial0/1/0 interface_state = "Down" and no neighbors

data.intent_description: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively down, causing the serial link to go down on R2 and R1 (R1 oper state = lower-layer-down). Remediate by bringing R2 Serial0/1/0 admin state to up to restore the link and OSPF adjacency.

plan.problem: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively shut (running-config contains 'shutdown'), resulting in R2 oper_state=down and R1 oper_state=lower-layer-down; OSPF adjacency on that interface is down.
plan.fix_summary: Clear administrative shutdown on R2 Serial0/1/0 (set admin_state to up) to restore the serial link and OSPF adjacency. Risk: brief interface flap and routing reconvergence during the change.

parsed.intent_description: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively down, causing the serial link to go down on R2 and R1 (R1 oper state = lower-layer-down). Remediate by bringing R2 Serial0/1/0 admin state to up to restore the link and OSPF adjacency.

data.intent_description: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively down, causing the serial link to go down on R2 and R1 (R1 oper state = lower-layer-down). Remediate by bringing R2 Serial0/1/0 admin state to up to restore the link and OSPF adjacency.

R1 Serial0/1/0 admin=up oper=lower-layer-down; R2 Serial0/1/0 admin=down oper=down
Both routers report OSPF interface Serial0/1/0 state=Down, no neighbors

root_causes.cause: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively shut, causing the link to go down on R2.
R1 Serial0/1/0 is administratively up but its line protocol went to lower-layer-down because the remote peer (R2) is administratively down/unavailable.
evidence: get_interface_status(router2): neighbor admin down (see above) — explains why R1's operational state dropped to lower-layer-down
get_ospf(router1): Serial0/1/0 interface_state = "Down" and no neighbors

plan.problem: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively shut (running-config contains 'shutdown'), resulting in R2 oper_state=down and R1 oper_state=lower-layer-down; OSPF adjacency on that interface is down.
plan.fix_summary: Clear administrative shutdown on R2 Serial0/1/0 (set admin_state to up) to restore the serial link and OSPF adjacency. Risk: brief interface flap and routing reconvergence during the change.

messages.content:
What was observed
- R2 Serial0/1/0 was administratively down (R2 oper=down) and R1 Serial0/1/0 was admin=up but oper=lower-layer-down. OSPF on that link was down with no neighbors.

Diagnosis (root cause)
- R2's Serial0/1/0 was shutdown in the running-config, causing the serial link to be down and preventing the OSPF adjacency.

If changes were made
- Cleared the shutdown on R2 Serial0/1/0 (set interface admin state = up).

Verification
- Passed. Post-change checks show R2 Serial0/1/0 admin=up oper=up and R1 Serial0/1/0 admin=up oper=up. OSPF on the link is Point-to-Point and the neighbor relationship is FULL on both routers.
- Remaining issues: none.

If changes were made

Verification
- Passed. Post-change checks show R2 Serial0/1/0 admin=up oper=up and R1 Serial0/1/0 admin=up oper=up. OSPF on the link is Point-to-Point and the neighbor relationship is FULL on both routers.
- Remaining issues: none.
